Libraries and Dataset Import¶

In [1]:
import pandas as pd

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from pandas.plotting import register_matplotlib_converters 
register_matplotlib_converters() 
%matplotlib inline

from statsmodels.tsa.seasonal import seasonal_decompose
from pmdarima.arima import auto_arima
from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error
In [2]:
df = pd.read_excel(r"problem to solve.xlsx",
                  sheet_name="DATA",
                  skiprows=5) #First rows are dataset details
df.head()

Data Transformation¶

Date Transformation¶

In [5]:
# Creating new date field by combining year+month and converting into date type
df["Date"] = df["Year"].astype(str) + "-" + df["Month"].astype(str)
df['Date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['Date'])
#transforming into Index for creation of Time Series 
df = df.set_index("Date")

df.head()

Time Series Seasonal Decomposition¶

Trend, Sasonality and Residual¶

In [18]:
ds_candy_bar1 = seasonal_decompose(ts_candy_bar1)
ds_candy_bar2 = seasonal_decompose(ts_candy_bar2)
ds_candy_bar3 = seasonal_decompose(ts_candy_bar3)
ds_candy_bar_total = seasonal_decompose(ts_candy_bar_total)
In [19]:
#trend
trend_candy_bar1 = ds_candy_bar1.trend
trend_candy_bar2 = ds_candy_bar2.trend
trend_candy_bar3 = ds_candy_bar3.trend
trend_candy_bar_total = ds_candy_bar_total.trend
In [20]:
#seasonal
seasonal_candy_bar1 = ds_candy_bar1.seasonal
seasonal_candy_bar2 = ds_candy_bar2.seasonal
seasonal_candy_bar3 = ds_candy_bar3.seasonal
seasonal_candy_bar_total = ds_candy_bar_total.seasonal
In [21]:
#resid
resid_candy_bar1 = ds_candy_bar1.resid
resid_candy_bar2 = ds_candy_bar2.resid
resid_candy_bar3 = ds_candy_bar3.resid
resid_candy_bar_total = ds_candy_bar_total.resid

Visualization by Brand¶

In [22]:
def graph_by_brand(orig, trend, seasonal, resid, title):
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(nrows=4,ncols=1, figsize=(8,5))

    ax[0].plot(orig, label = "Original")
    ax[1].plot(trend, label = "Trend")
    ax[2].plot(seasonal, label = "Seasonality")
    ax[3].plot(resid, label = "Residual")
    
    ax[0].legend(loc = 'best')
    ax[1].legend(loc = 'best')
    ax[2].legend(loc = 'best')
    ax[3].legend(loc = 'best')
    
    ax[0].set_title(title)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
In [23]:
graph_by_brand(ts_candy_bar1, 
               trend_candy_bar1, 
               seasonal_candy_bar1, 
               resid_candy_bar1, 
               "Candy Bars Brand 1")
In [24]:
graph_by_brand(ts_candy_bar2, 
               trend_candy_bar2, 
               seasonal_candy_bar2, 
               resid_candy_bar2, 
               "Candy Bars Brand 2")
In [25]:
graph_by_brand(ts_candy_bar3, 
               trend_candy_bar3, 
               seasonal_candy_bar3, 
               resid_candy_bar3, 
               "Candy Bars Brand 3")
In [26]:
graph_by_brand(ts_candy_bar_total, 
               trend_candy_bar_total, 
               seasonal_candy_bar_total, 
               resid_candy_bar_total, 
               "Candy Bars Total Brands")

Visualization comparing Brands¶

In [27]:
def graph_by_element(brand1, brand2, brand3, brand_total, title):
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(nrows=4,ncols=1, figsize=(8,5))
    
    ax[0].plot(brand1, label = "Brand 1")
    ax[1].plot(brand2, label = "Brand 2")
    ax[2].plot(brand3, label = "Brand 3")
    ax[3].plot(brand_total, label = "Total")
    
    ax[0].legend(loc = 'best')
    ax[1].legend(loc = 'best')
    ax[2].legend(loc = 'best')
    ax[3].legend(loc = 'best')
    
    ax[0].set_title(title)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
In [28]:
graph_by_element(ts_candy_bar1,
                 ts_candy_bar2,
                 ts_candy_bar3,
                 ts_candy_bar_total,
                 "Original")
In [29]:
graph_by_element(trend_candy_bar1,
                 trend_candy_bar2,
                 trend_candy_bar3,
                 trend_candy_bar_total,
                 "Trend")
In [30]:
graph_by_element(seasonal_candy_bar1,
                 seasonal_candy_bar2,
                 seasonal_candy_bar3,
                 seasonal_candy_bar_total,
                 "Seasonality")
In [31]:
graph_by_element(resid_candy_bar1,
                 resid_candy_bar2,
                 resid_candy_bar3,
                 resid_candy_bar_total,
                 "Residual")

Arima Model Forecast¶

Model Definition¶

In [32]:
def arima_predict(ts, periods = 12):
    # identify optimal p, d, q parameters
    arima_model = auto_arima(ts, 
                               # d and D = 1 since data is not stationary (not constant, they float along time)
                               #p and q starting from  1, default is bigger, more recent has more impact
                               start_p=1, d=1, start_q=1, 
                               start_P=1, D=1, start_Q=1,
                               max_p=6, max_q=6, #limiting possibilities 
                               m=12, #historical data has monthly format
                               seasonal=True, # data is seasonal after analyzing graphs above
                               trace=True, #show attempts
                               stepwise=True) #optimize possibilities, False didn't present better results
    #AIC minimization
    print("AIC: " + arima_model.aic().astype(str))

    #splitting into train and test sets
    train = ts.loc["2014-01-01":"2016-06-01"]
    test  = ts.loc["2016-07-01":]

    #training
    arima_model.fit(train)

    #predict test set and forecast 2017 and 2018
    forecast = arima_model.predict(n_periods=periods)
    forecast = pd.DataFrame(forecast, columns=["Forecast"])

    #comparing test and predict
    forecast_compare = pd.DataFrame(forecast,index = test.index,columns=["Forecast"])

    #mean absolute error (MAE)
    mae = mean_absolute_error(test, forecast_compare)
    print("MAE: " + str(mae))

    #graphs
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(nrows=2,ncols=1, figsize=(8,5))
    
    ax[0].plot(pd.concat([test,forecast_compare],axis=1))
    ax[1].plot(pd.concat([ts,forecast],axis=1))
    
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
    
    return forecast, arima_model

Candy Bars Brand 1 Predict¶

In [33]:
forecast1, arima_model1 = arima_predict(ts_candy_bar1, periods = 30)

Performing stepwise search to minimize aic
 ARIMA(1,1,1)(1,1,1)[12]             : AIC=570.426, Time=0.14 sec

Results and Analysis¶

Generating Final Dataset¶

In [36]:
df_predict = df_predict.copy()
df_predict['Candy Bar Brand 1'] = forecast1["2017-08-01":].values
df_predict['Candy Bar Brand 2'] = forecast2["2017-08-01":].values
df_predict['Candy Bar Brand 3 Complete'] = forecast3["2017-08-01":].values
df_predict["Candy Bars Total Brands"] = df_predict['Candy Bar Brand 1'] + df_predict['Candy Bar Brand 2'] + df_predict['Candy Bar Brand 3 Complete']

Historical and Forecast Combined¶

In [39]:
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(15,8))

for location in ['right','left','top','bottom']:
  ax.spines[location].set_visible(False)

ax.tick_params(bottom=False, left=False)

ax.plot(df_final.index,df_final["Candy Bar Brand 1"], label="Brand 1", alpha=0.4, color="goldenrod")
ax.plot(df_final.index,df_final["Candy Bar Brand 2"], label="Brand 2", alpha=0.4, color="blue")
ax.plot(df_final.index,df_final["Candy Bar Brand 3 Complete"], label="Brand 3", alpha=0.4, color="orangered")
ax.plot(df_final.index,df_final["Candy Bars Total Brands"], label="Total", alpha=0.4, color="purple")

ax.plot(df_final.loc["2017-08-01":].index,df_final["Candy Bar Brand 1"].loc["2017-08-01":], alpha=0.8, color="goldenrod")
ax.plot(df_final.loc["2017-08-01":].index,df_final["Candy Bar Brand 2"].loc["2017-08-01":], alpha=0.8, color="blue")
ax.plot(df_final.loc["2017-08-01":].index,df_final["Candy Bar Brand 3 Complete"].loc["2017-08-01":], alpha=0.8, color="orangered")
ax.plot(df_final.loc["2017-08-01":].index,df_final["Candy Bars Total Brands"].loc["2017-08-01":], alpha=0.8, color="purple")

ax.axvline(x="2017-08-01", linestyle='--', alpha=0.4, color='purple')
ax.text("2017-09-01",15000,'Forecast', size=12)

ax.text("2014-02-01", 22000,'Brazil Candy Bars Brands Sales in tons', size=15, weight='bold')
plt.legend(loc = 'best')
plt.show()

Market Share¶

In [40]:
df_final["Share Brand 1"] = round(df_final["Candy Bar Brand 1"] / df_final["Candy Bars Total Brands"],2) * 100
df_final["Share Brand 2"] = round(df_final["Candy Bar Brand 2"] / df_final["Candy Bars Total Brands"],2) * 100
df_final["Share Brand 3"] = round(df_final["Candy Bar Brand 3 Complete"] / df_final["Candy Bars Total Brands"],2) * 100

In [41]:
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(15,7))

for location in ['right','left','top','bottom']:
  ax.spines[location].set_visible(False)

ax.tick_params(bottom=False, left=False)

ax.plot(df_final.index,df_final["Share Brand 1"], label="Brand 1", alpha=0.4, color="goldenrod")
ax.plot(df_final.index,df_final["Share Brand 2"], label="Brand 2", alpha=0.4, color="blue")
ax.plot(df_final.index,df_final["Share Brand 3"], label="Brand 3", alpha=0.4, color="orangered")

ax.plot(df_final.loc["2017-08-01":].index,df_final["Share Brand 1"].loc["2017-08-01":], alpha=0.8, color="goldenrod")
ax.plot(df_final.loc["2017-08-01":].index,df_final["Share Brand 2"].loc["2017-08-01":], alpha=0.8, color="blue")
ax.plot(df_final.loc["2017-08-01":].index,df_final["Share Brand 3"].loc["2017-08-01":], alpha=0.8, color="orangered")

ax.axvline(x="2017-08-01", linestyle='--', alpha=0.4, color='purple')
ax.text("2017-09-01",80,'Forecast', size=12)

ax.text("2014-02-01", 100,'% Brazil Market Share Candy Bars Brands', size=15, weight='bold')
plt.legend(loc = 'best')
plt.show()

Brand Growth¶

In [42]:
df_final["Brand 1 Growth"] = df_final["Candy Bar Brand 1"].pct_change(fill_method=None)
df_final["Brand 2 Growth"] = df_final["Candy Bar Brand 2"].pct_change(fill_method=None)
df_final["Brand 3 Growth"] = df_final["Candy Bar Brand 3 Complete"].pct_change(fill_method=None)

In [43]:
columns = ["Year","Candy Bar Brand 1","Candy Bar Brand 2", "Candy Bar Brand 3 Complete"]
annual = df_final[columns].groupby("Year").sum()
annual["Brand 1 Growth"] = annual["Candy Bar Brand 1"].pct_change() * 100
annual["Brand 2 Growth"] = annual["Candy Bar Brand 2"].pct_change() * 100
annual["Brand 3 Growth"] = annual["Candy Bar Brand 3 Complete"].pct_change() * 100

annual.head()

Candy Bar Brand 1 Candy Bar Brand 2 Candy Bar Brand 3 Complete Brand 1 Growth Brand 2 Growth Brand 3 Growth
Year
2014 73567.511277 25102.588902 16265.846230 NaN NaN NaN

2016 62948.080435 24163.554026 13614.486677 -5.322360 0.498657 2.319891

In [44]:
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,5))

for location in ['right','left','top','bottom']:
  ax.spines[location].set_visible(False)

ax.tick_params(bottom=False, left=False)

ax.set_xticks([2015,2016, 2017, 2018])
ax.set_xticklabels(['2015','2016','2017','2018'])

ax.plot(annual["Brand 1 Growth"], label="Brand 1", alpha=0.4, color="goldenrod")
ax.plot(annual["Brand 2 Growth"], label="Brand 2", alpha=0.4, color="blue")
ax.plot(annual["Brand 3 Growth"], label="Brand 3", alpha=0.4, color="orangered")

ax.text(2015, 10,'% Brazil Annual Growth Candy Bars Brands', size=10, weight='bold')
plt.legend(loc = 'best')
plt.show()

Monthly Sales Performance¶

In [45]:
columns = ["Month","Candy Bar Brand 1","Candy Bar Brand 2", "Candy Bar Brand 3 Complete"]
monthly = df_final[columns].groupby("Month").mean()

match_month = {"Jan" : 1, "Feb" : 2, "Mar" : 3,
               "Apr" : 4, "May" : 5, "Jun" : 6,
               "Jul" : 7, "Aug" : 8, "Sep" : 9,
               "Oct" : 10, "Nov" : 11, "Dec" : 12}

monthly["Month #"] =  monthly.index.map(match_month)
monthly = monthly.sort_values(by=['Month #'])

monthly
Out[45]:

In [46]:
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,5))

for location in ['right','left','top','bottom']:
  ax.spines[location].set_visible(False)

ax.tick_params(bottom=False, left=False)

ax.plot(monthly["Candy Bar Brand 1"], label="Brand 1", alpha=0.4, color="goldenrod")
ax.plot(monthly["Candy Bar Brand 2"], label="Brand 2", alpha=0.4, color="blue")
ax.plot(monthly["Candy Bar Brand 3 Complete"], label="Brand 3", alpha=0.4, color="orangered")

ax.text(3, 10000,'Average Sales in tons by Month', size=10, weight='bold')
plt.legend(loc = 'best')
plt.show()

Writing Final Dataset¶

In [47]:
df_final.to_csv("problem_solved.csv", sep = ";", float_format='%g')
